title: Humboltia incompta
taxonomy: Humboltia incompta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. This plant is part of the genus Humboldtia, which belongs to the family Apocynaceae. The classification of Humboltia incompta is further detailed as follows: Humboltia incompta is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the clade Angiosperms, in the clade Eudicots, in the order Gentianales, in the family Apocynaceae, in the genus Humboldtia, and in the species H. incompta. Further classification information may be available in botanical resources.

intro: Humboldtia incompta, also known as the "Kundang," is a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of Borneo. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia incompta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Humboldtia incompta is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of capsules or tinctures, to utilize its medicinal benefits.
